full_content:
Verbatim owner statements (from S330 chat, 2026-05-04):

Statement 1:
"All GT-KB files must be in this directory: E:\GT-KB
All files in the E:\GT-KB directory are part of GT-KB *except* the files which are deployed within the E:\GT-KB\applications sub-directory.
All Agent Red files must be in this directory: E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red
The *only* files within the E:\GT-KB\applications\ sub-directory are deployed applications, each within a named sub-directory, as is the case for Agent_Red"

Statement 2 (clarification on internal subdirectories):
"I do not care about the creation of sub-directories within the GT-KB root (E:\GT-KB). You should not flatten the structures unless that is the correct choice for reasons of usability and comprehensibility. Create sub-directories as required in order to logically arrange the GT-KB directory structure and content. The only path that may *not* be altered is E:\GT-KB\applications\."

Statement 3 (separation framing):
"Agent Red is *not* a part of GT-KB. We have included 4 small demo apps. Agent Red should be pushed to https://github.com/mike-remakerdigital/agent-red and GroundTruth-KB should be pushed to https://github.com/Remaker-Digital/groundtruth-kb. These are now separate projects. That was the point of the GT-KB isolation project."

Decision (5 binding rules):

RULE 1 (root containment): All GT-KB project files MUST live within E:\GT-KB. No GT-KB artifact may be created, read, written, verified, or required from outside that root.

RULE 2 (GT-KB-vs-application partition): Everything in E:\GT-KB\ is GT-KB platform EXCEPT the contents of E:\GT-KB\applications\, which are deployed applications (separate projects with their own identity, repo, and lifecycle).

RULE 3 (Agent Red location): All Agent Red project files MUST live within E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\. Agent Red is a deployed application, not a part of GT-KB.

RULE 4 (applications/ namespace purity): The E:\GT-KB\applications\ subdirectory contains ONLY named sub-directories, each holding one deployed application. No other files or directories belong at applications/ root.

RULE 5 (internal organization flexibility): Subdirectory organization within E:\GT-KB\ root (outside of applications/) is at the AI's discretion based on usability and comprehensibility. Don't flatten unless flattening is the right choice. The applications/ path itself is the only path that may not be altered or restructured.

Repo topology (binding):
- E:\GT-KB\.git remote: https://github.com/Remaker-Digital/groundtruth-kb
- E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\.git remote: https://github.com/mike-remakerdigital/agent-red
- These are independent git repositories nested in the directory tree (not a monorepo, not submodules).

Why this is being captured RETROACTIVELY in S330:
This rule was stated by the owner during GT-KB isolation project planning (per owner recollection in S330 chat). The captured planning artifacts (DELIB-0877, DELIB-0878, DELIB-0879) presented topology options A/B/C and recommended sibling-folder layouts; DELIB-0879 explicitly REJECTED the owner's actual choice (Option A as monorepo). The owner's verbatim choice was never captured as a source_type=owner_conversation DELIB. CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md were updated to reflect the correct rules but with no DELIB tying the rule files to the owner's original statement.

Per Slice 8.6 incident analysis (owner-requested governance audit, S330): this capture gap is the root cause of multi-session work on the wrong target (Agent Red CI), and will be the canonical proof case for the just-approved DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION. Had that hook existed during planning, the owner's verbatim topology choice would have been captured as a DELIB at the moment of stating it.

Reconciliation with prior captured artifacts:
- SUPERSEDES the planning recommendations in DELIB-0879 to the extent they conflict (DELIB-0879's Option B and rejection of monorepo Option A no longer apply; the owner's actual choice is the captured-Option-A-as-nested-monorepo form).
- REINFORCES CLAUDE.md "Mandatory Project Root Boundary" section and .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md (these rule files correctly state the rule; this DELIB is their now-explicit source-of-truth owner conversation).
- Will be referenced as the source DELIB by a new GOV spec (proposed: GOV-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-001).

Implementation gap surfaced (NOT this DELIB's scope, but flagged):
The rules describe the END STATE. The CURRENT STATE violates the rules: Agent Red files (admin/, widget/, src/agents/, src/multi_tenant/, Dockerfile*, infrastructure/, tests/, requirements*.txt, package.json, README.md, etc.) live AT E:\GT-KB\ root, not inside E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\. The actual file MIGRATION work was never scoped into ISOLATION-017. A new bridge thread (proposed: GTKB-ISOLATION-018-AGENT-RED-FILE-MIGRATION) is required to perform the migration.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
